evals.name: build-plan-parallel-default
evals.prompt: Build the plan in .specs/plans/2026-05-22-implement_journal_app/ (Layout: kanban). It has six tasks (01 passphrase lock, 02 entry store, 03 app shell, 04 editor, 05 calendar, 06 export) with a number-keyed dependency table; each task file sits in backlog/ beside its co-located NN-…-certificate.md. Implement it.
evals.expected_output: The skill resolves settings to the defaults (parallel, max 4 agents, gate_mode combined) and echoes them. It detects the kanban layout (the Layout: kanban marker / a backlog/ subfolder) and enumerates the task set as the union of backlog/, in-progress/, blocked/, and done/ (a missing subfolder is empty). It reads plan.md's dependency TABLE as the source of truth (not the Mermaid graph), keyed by task number, builds the DAG, and sanity-checks it. Using the vendored workspaces.md method it detects the VCS backend (jj preferred when both jj and git exist, else git) and announces the choice; it establishes a clean base, an integration point (a jj revision or the spec-builder/integration git branch), and one workspace per task branched from the current integration point. It runs a wave scheduler: tasks 01 and 02 (no deps) dispatch concurrently up to the cap; each later task dispatches only once its Depends-on tasks are in done/ (merged). On dispatch it moves a task (and its co-located certificate) from backlog/ into in-progress/ on the main tree. Each task gets a context-sized brief built from its own Implements/Produces/Pointers/Steps/Definition-of-done (NOT the whole spec or other tasks' files; the certificate body is withheld from the implementer). When a sub-agent reports done, the orchestrator dispatches a single verifier (gate_mode: combined, the default) — a different agent than the implementer — that reads the diff once and returns both verdicts, correctness (semi-formal-review → CORRECT/.../BUGGY) and completeness (validate-done-certificate, discharging the task's co-located in-progress/NN-…-certificate.md read from the main tree against the diff → DONE/PARTIAL/NOT_DONE), running the shared checkpoints once. Only when correctness is CORRECT/LIKELY_CORRECT AND completeness is DONE does the task merge into the integration point, the task file and its certificate move from in-progress/ into done/ on the main tree with steps checked off, the certificate State set to Validated, and its workspace torn down. plan.md's Status is recomputed from the subfolders — In progress once any task has left backlog/, Done only when every task is in done/. A final build summary reports per-task verdicts.

evals.name: build-sequential-with-failed-gate
evals.prompt: Implement .specs/plans/2026-05-24-add_tagging/ sequentially, one task at a time. Max one agent.
evals.expected_output: execution_mode resolves to sequential (max_parallel_agents = 1); the skill echoes that. The wave scheduler dispatches exactly one ready task at a time (moving it from backlog/ into in-progress/ on the main tree) and fully resolves it (including merge and the move into done/) before the next, using the same build loop. For each task: implement in an isolated workspace, then a single verifier runs the combined correctness+completeness gate (gate_mode: combined, the default — one agent, both verdicts over one reading of the diff). When either verdict fails — e.g. correctness (semi-formal-review) returns BUGGY on a name-shadowing or regression finding, or completeness (validate-done-certificate) returns NOT_DONE/PARTIAL on an UNSATISFIED obligation — the skill does NOT move the task into done/. It re-dispatches the same implementer in the same workspace, briefed with the specific verdict/evidence (the offending file:line, the unmet DoD item), re-runs the verification, and bounds retries (default 2). After the bound without both verdicts passing, it parks the task — moving it (and its certificate) from in-progress/ into blocked/ with a **Blocked:** <reason> line, workspace left intact — and surfaces the diff and standing verdicts to the user rather than forcing a pass or looping. Tasks depending on a parked task are reported as blocked (an ls blocked/ plus the dependents that transitively need it). The bar is never lowered to go green.

evals.name: no-plan-redirect-and-resume
evals.prompt: We have a spec at .specs/journal/specs/ but no plan yet. Build it. (Also: later, resume .specs/plans/2026-05-22-implement_journal_app/ where tasks 01-03 are already in done/.)
evals.expected_output: For the spec-with-no-plan request, the skill declines to build straight from a spec and redirects to spec-planner to produce the dependency-ordered plan (with definitions of done) first, because decomposition and reviewability ordering are prerequisites for a reviewable build. For the resume request, it reads folder membership, treats 01-03 (already in done/) as preconditions already in the integration point rather than rebuilding them, restarts the wave scheduler from the current ready set (the backlog/ tasks whose dependencies are all in done/), and continues the build loop and folder bookkeeping from there. It does not re-dispatch or re-gate finished tasks.

evals.name: build-in-plain-git-repo
evals.prompt: Build .specs/plans/2026-06-01-add_search/ in this repo. It's a plain git repo, not jj.
evals.expected_output: Backend detection (vendored workspaces.md) finds no jj repo and falls back to the git backend, announcing it. The integration point is a git branch (spec-builder/integration) created from a clean base; each task gets its own git worktree on a spec-builder/task-NN branch off the current integration tip (`git worktree add -b spec-builder/task-NN ../<repo>-task-NN spec-builder/integration`), with deps installed and the suite run green before the sub-agent starts. Merging a completed task advances the integration branch (`git merge --no-ff spec-builder/task-NN`); parallel tasks branching from the same tip may conflict on the second merge and are handled (not auto-resolved silently) with the verification re-run on the merged result. Teardown is `git worktree remove` + `git branch -d`. Everything else — the verification, the wave scheduler, status bookkeeping — is identical to the jj path; no jj commands are used. If the repo were colocated (both .jj and .git), the skill would instead prefer jj.
